text:
When you write code, you will inevitably encounter bugs. Identifying bugs is the first step in fixing them. A systematic approach helps you avoid guessing randomly. Start by reading error messages carefully, then isolate the problematic section.

Debugging tools like breakpoints allow you to pause your code and inspect variables. Another helpful tool is logging. By adding log statements, you can track your program's flow and see where values go wrong. Logging is especially useful when a bug only appears in certain conditions.

Remember, patience is key. Break the problem into small parts, test each one, and use your tools wisely. This method will save you hours of frustration.
